categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَدُلُّ عَلَى الْعُلُوِّ أَوِ الْاِرْتِفَاعِ
categories.nameEn: Words for high places or elevation

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًۭا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ
items.verseEn: And We have placed within the heaven great stars and have beautified it for the observers.
Exalt the name of your Lord, the Most High,

items.verse: وَجَعَلْنَا ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُۥٓ ءَايَةًۭ وَءَاوَيْنَـٰهُمَآ إِلَىٰ رَبْوَةٍۢ ذَاتِ قَرَارٍۢ وَمَعِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign and sheltered them within a high ground having level [areas] and flowing water.
And have shown him the two ways?

items.verse: وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةًۭ ثُمَّ نَزَعْنَـٰهَا مِنْهُ إِنَّهُۥ لَيَـُٔوسٌۭ كَفُورٌۭ
items.verseEn: And if We give man a taste of mercy from Us and then We withdraw it from him, indeed, he is despairing and ungrateful.

items.verse: وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَـٰهُ نَعْمَآءَ بَعْدَ ضَرَّآءَ مَسَّتْهُ لَيَقُولَنَّ ذَهَبَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتُ عَنِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَفَرِحٌۭ فَخُورٌ
items.verseEn: But if We give him a taste of favor after hardship has touched him, he will surely say, "Bad times have left me." Indeed, he is exultant and boastful -
And when good touches him, withholding [of it],

categories.name: مُصْطَلَحَاتٌ فِقْهِيَّةٌ وَاجْتِمَاعِيَّةٌ
categories.nameEn: Terms related to Islamic social and legal rulings (Fiqh)

items.verse: قَدْ سَمِعَ ٱللَّهُ قَوْلَ ٱلَّتِى تُجَـٰدِلُكَ فِى زَوْجِهَا وَتَشْتَكِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَٱللَّهُ يَسْمَعُ تَحَاوُرَكُمَآ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌۢ بَصِيرٌ
items.verseEn: Certainly has Allāh heard the speech of the one who argues [i.e., pleads] with you, [O Muḥammad], concerning her husband and directs her complaint to Allāh. And Allāh hears your dialogue; indeed, Allāh is Hearing and Seeing.

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ يُظَـٰهِرُونَ مِن نِّسَآئِهِمْ ثُمَّ يَعُودُونَ لِمَا قَالُوا۟ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍۢ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَتَمَآسَّا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ تُوعَظُونَ بِهِۦ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: And those who pronounce ẓihār from their wives and then [wish to] go back on what they said - then [there must be] the freeing of a slave before they touch one another. That is what you are admonished thereby; and Allāh is Aware of what you do.

items.en: call each other by offensive names
items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا يَسْخَرْ قَوْمٌۭ مِّن قَوْمٍ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونُوا۟ خَيْرًۭا مِّنْهُمْ وَلَا نِسَآءٌۭ مِّن نِّسَآءٍ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُنَّ خَيْرًۭا مِّنْهُنَّ ۖ وَلَا تَلْمِزُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَلَا تَنَابَزُوا۟ بِٱلْأَلْقَـٰبِ ۖ بِئْسَ ٱلِٱسْمُ ٱلْفُسُوقُ بَعْدَ ٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَتُبْ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, let not a people ridicule [another] people; perhaps they may be better than them; nor let women ridicule [other] women; perhaps they may be better than them. And do not insult one another and do not call each other by [offensive] nicknames. Wretched is the name [i.e., mention] of disobedience after [one's] faith. And whoever does not repent - then it is those who are the wrongdoers.

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نَكَحْتُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ فَمَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍۢ تَعْتَدُّونَهَا ۖ فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَرَاحًۭا جَمِيلًۭا
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, when you marry believing women and then divorce them before you have touched them [i.e., consummated the marriage], then there is not for you any waiting period to count concerning them. So provide for them and give them a gracious release.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ وَرَدَتْ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Words that appear only once in the Quran (Hapax Legomena)

items.verse: وَٱلْقَمَرَ قَدَّرْنَـٰهُ مَنَازِلَ حَتَّىٰ عَادَ كَٱلْعُرْجُونِ ٱلْقَدِيمِ
items.verseEn: And the moon - We have determined for it phases, until it returns [appearing] like the old date stalk.

items.verse: وَأَنزَلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَـٰهَرُوهُم مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِن صَيَاصِيهِمْ وَقَذَفَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلرُّعْبَ فَرِيقًۭا تَقْتُلُونَ وَتَأْسِرُونَ فَرِيقًۭا
items.verseEn: And He brought down those who supported them among the People of the Scripture from their fortresses and cast terror into their hearts [so that] a party [i.e., their men] you killed, and you took captive a party [i.e., the women and children].
And lofty palm trees having fruit arranged in layers -

items.verse: فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنَا وَأَهْلَنَا ٱلضُّرُّ وَجِئْنَا بِبِضَـٰعَةٍۢ مُّزْجَىٰةٍۢ فَأَوْفِ لَنَا ٱلْكَيْلَ وَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَيْنَآ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَجْزِى ٱلْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ
items.verseEn: So when they entered upon him [i.e., Joseph], they said, "O ʿAzeez, adversity has touched us and our family, and we have come with goods poor in quality, but give us full measure and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allāh rewards the charitable."

harf.hint: An idol worshipped by the people of Prophet Ilyas; also a term for a husband.
harf.verse: 37:125 — Do you call upon Baʿl and leave the best of creators -

deduction.title: The Voice of a Plaintive Plea
deduction.intro: In a time of established customs, one soul found themselves in a distressing situation, their family life suddenly upended by a single statement. Unwilling to accept this fate in silence, this individual took their case to the highest authority, speaking directly to the Prophet (ﷺ). Their heartfelt plea was not unheard, leading to a divine revelation that would clarify a law and provide a path to resolution for all time. Can you identify this figure whose voice reached the heavens?
clues: I was bound by a cultural practice of my time, one that left me in a state of uncertainty and distress.
A statement uttered by my husband in anger rendered me like a mother to him, yet I was not divorced.
With my children's future at stake, I sought counsel and justice from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).
I debated my case earnestly, pleading with the Prophet (ﷺ), but the existing customs offered no clear solution.
I raised my complaint to Allah directly, and my plea was heard from above the seven heavens.
A new ruling was revealed because of my situation, detailed in the opening verses of the surah named for one who pleads or disputes.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The wife of Abu Lahab
A woman who made a false accusation against her husband
The woman who disputed regarding her husband
The mother of the believers, 'Aisha
A woman who tested Prophet Sulayman
identity.answer: The woman who disputed regarding her husband

trial.label: What was my specific issue?
options: A dispute over inheritance
Seeking a divorce through 'Khul'
The breaking of an oath ('Kaffarah')
The practice of 'Dhihar'
A false accusation of theft

location.label: What was the core of my plea?
options: A demand for the return of a dowry
A request for a miraculous sign
A complaint about a stolen camel
The welfare of my young children
A prophecy about an upcoming battle
location.answer: The welfare of my young children

outcome.label: What was the result of my plea?
options: A divine law was revealed, condemning the practice and prescribing an expiation
I was told to be patient and accept my fate
My husband was exiled from the city
A verse was revealed permitting all pre-Islamic divorce practices
The Prophet (ﷺ) judged using his personal wisdom alone
outcome.answer: A divine law was revealed, condemning the practice and prescribing an expiation

deduction.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُظَـٰهِرُونَ مِنكُم مِّن نِّسَآئِهِم مَّا هُنَّ أُمَّهَـٰتِهِمْ ۖ إِنْ أُمَّهَـٰتُهُمْ إِلَّا ٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى وَلَدْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَقُولُونَ مُنكَرًۭا مِّنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ وَزُورًۭا ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَفُوٌّ غَفُورٌۭ
deduction.verse: Those who pronounce ẓihār among you [to separate] from their wives - they are not [consequently] their mothers. Their mothers are none but those who gave birth to them. And indeed, they are saying an objectionable statement and a falsehood. But indeed, Allāh is Pardoning and Forgiving. (58:2)

translations: He (is) the One Who created the heavens and the earth in six periods
then He rose over the Throne He knows what penetrates
in(to) the earth and what comes forth from it and what descends
from the heaven and what ascends therein and He (is) with you
wherever wherever you are And Allah of what you do (is) All-seer
scramble.hint: A powerful statement on divine omnipresence and complete awareness of all actions.
scramble.arabic: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍۢ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۖ وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌۭ
words: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍۢ
ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ
فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ
مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۖ وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ
أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌۭ
scramble.verseEn: It is He who created the heavens and earth in six days and then established Himself above the Throne. He knows what penetrates into the earth and what emerges from it and what descends from the heaven and what ascends therein; and He is with you wherever you are. And Allāh, of what you do, is Seeing.
